entries.problem: api/discord.js calls .json() on CoinGecko OHLC response without checking content-type, risking SSE/stream parse errors
entries.commit: fix: guard CoinGecko OHLC fetch against SSE/non-JSON responses in discord.js getQuickSignal
entries.learned: CoinGecko (like Yahoo Finance) can return non-JSON on rate limits or errors, so every external fetch must validate content-type before calling .json().

entries.learned: CoinGecko fetch calls were unguarded in trading-expert.js while the same pattern was already fixed in discord.js — the fix needs to be applied consistently across all files that consume CoinGecko responses.

entries.problem: api/morning.js fetchMarkets() calls .json() directly without checking response status or content-type, risking parse errors on non-JSON responses
entries.commit: fix: add content-type check and error logging to fetchMarkets() in api/morning.js
entries.learned: Empty catch blocks are consistently used across this codebase, hiding errors silently — every fetch helper needs both content-type validation and at minimum a console.warn in the catch clause.

entries.note: Patterns detected before Claude analysis — guiding fix priority

entries.problem: fetch() call in api/brain.js to Anthropic API (askBrain) has AbortSignal.timeout(30000) but the .json() call after checking r.ok has no error handling — if Claude returns a non-JSON body (e.g. rate limit HTML page), it will throw an unhandled error crashing the Brain orchestrator
entries.commit: fix: guard askBrain() against non-JSON Anthropic responses crashing Brain orchestrator
entries.learned: Critical orchestration functions like askBrain() must defensively check content-type and catch .json() parse errors, because upstream API errors (rate limits, maintenance pages) often return HTML/text bodies that will throw SyntaxError and silently kill the entire Brain cycle.
